final-neighborhood-resource-guide-histor.label: Final Capstone: Neighborhood Guide History
final-neighborhood-resource-guide-histor.summary: Build a fresh neighborhood-guide repository through six cumulative capstone steps.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Clean Tracked Paths and Recover a Mistake
Commit and Audit the Final Handoff
Initialize and Record the Guide Baseline
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt: Inside `neighborhood-guide/`, initialize a repository on `main` and store the supplied local author identity. Stage the four baseline text files—`README.md`, `services.txt`, `hours.txt`, and `old-contacts.txt`—then record them with the message `Start neighborhood guide`.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Build the first snapshot from the four supplied text files.

help.concept: The capstone starts independently; the files are supplied, but the Git workflow is yours to apply.
help.hint_1: Use main and the exact local identity.
help.hint_2: Use the exact first commit message.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.starterCode:
# Neighborhood Guide

Local services and opening hours.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
git init -b main
git config --local user.name "ZoeSkoul Learner"
git config --local user.email "learner@zoeskoul.local"
git add README.md services.txt hours.txt old-contacts.txt
git commit -m "Start neighborhood guide"
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions: Inside `neighborhood-guide/`, initialize a repository on `main` and store the supplied local author identity. Stage the four baseline text files—`README.md`, `services.txt`, `hours.txt`, and `old-contacts.txt`—then record them with the message `Start neighborhood guide`.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- neighborhood-guide
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
printf %s '# Neighborhood Guide

Local services and opening hours.
' > neighborhood-guide/README.md
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
printf %s 'Neighborhood services

Welcome desk
Food pantry
' > neighborhood-guide/services.txt
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
printf %s 'Help desk hours: 10 AM - 4 PM
' > neighborhood-guide/hours.txt
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
printf %s 'Old contact list

Retired phone tree.
' > neighborhood-guide/old-contacts.txt

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -eu
rm -rf -- neighborhood-guide
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
git -C neighborhood-guide init -q -b main
git -C neighborhood-guide config --local user.name 'ZoeSkoul Learner'
git -C neighborhood-guide config --local user.email learner@zoeskoul.local
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
printf %s '# Neighborhood Guide

Local services and opening hours.
' > neighborhood-guide/README.md
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
printf %s 'Neighborhood services

Welcome desk
Food pantry
' > neighborhood-guide/services.txt
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
printf %s 'Help desk hours: 10 AM - 4 PM
' > neighborhood-guide/hours.txt
mkdir -p -- neighborhood-guide
printf %s 'Old contact list

Retired phone tree.
' > neighborhood-guide/old-contacts.txt
git -C neighborhood-guide add -A
GIT_AUTHOR_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z GIT_COMMITTER_DATE=2026-01-01T12:00:00Z git -C neighborhood-guide commit -q -m 'Start neighborhood guide'

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Update the Help Desk Hours
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt: Open `neighborhood-guide/hours.txt`. Replace `Help desk hours: 10 AM - 4 PM` with `Help desk hours: 9 AM - 5 PM`. Save the file, inspect its unstaged change, stage it, and record it with the message `Update help desk hours`.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Review the exact hours change before adding it to history.

help.concept: An exact text edit becomes a focused capstone commit.
help.hint_1: Do not change the filename or add other text.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
git diff -- hours.txt
git add hours.txt
git commit -m "Update help desk hours"
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions: Open `neighborhood-guide/hours.txt`. Replace `Help desk hours: 10 AM - 4 PM` with `Help desk hours: 9 AM - 5 PM`. Save the file, inspect its unstaged change, stage it, and record it with the message `Update help desk hours`.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Add the Generated-File Policy
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt: Create `neighborhood-guide/.gitignore` with exactly `generated/` on line 1. Save it, create `generated/preview.txt`, inspect status with ignored paths included, and stage only `.gitignore`.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: The preview stays local and ignored; the policy itself joins the next commit.

help.concept: The ignore policy is intentional project source; the generated preview is not.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.starterCode:
# Neighborhood Guide

Local services and opening hours.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
mkdir -p generated
touch generated/preview.txt
git status --short --ignored
git add .gitignore
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions: Create `neighborhood-guide/.gitignore` with exactly `generated/` on line 1. Save it, create `generated/preview.txt`, inspect status with ignored paths included, and stage only `.gitignore`.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Clean Paths and Recover a README Mistake
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt: Open `neighborhood-guide/README.md`. Replace `Local services and opening hours.` with `Accidental rewrite.` and save. Stage that mistake, rename tracked `services.txt` to `community-services.txt`, and remove tracked `old-contacts.txt`. Then unstage and restore only the README mistake. Inspect the staged name-status summary; it should contain the rename, removal, and `.gitignore`, but not README.md.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Keep the intended maintenance work staged while recovering the accidental README edit.

help.concept: Independent Git work means protecting the desired snapshot while removing one accidental change.
help.hint_2: Do not disturb the rename, removal, or .gitignore.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.starterCode:
# Neighborhood Guide

Local services and opening hours.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
git add README.md
git mv services.txt community-services.txt
git rm old-contacts.txt
git restore --staged README.md
git restore README.md
git diff --staged --name-status
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions: Open `neighborhood-guide/README.md`. Replace `Local services and opening hours.` with `Accidental rewrite.` and save. Stage that mistake, rename tracked `services.txt` to `community-services.txt`, and remove tracked `old-contacts.txt`. Then unstage and restore only the README mistake. Inspect the staged name-status summary; it should contain the rename, removal, and `.gitignore`, but not README.md.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.title: Commit and Audit the Final Handoff
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.prompt: Record the prepared maintenance snapshot with the message `Prepare neighborhood guide handoff`. Then inspect the concise history, the latest commit summary, and the final short status. The capstone should end with four focused commits and no unfinished tracked work.
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.hint: Verify the full timeline, the final snapshot, and the clean handoff state.

help.concept: The final handoff is proven by focused history, intentional tracked files, ignored generated output, and clean state.
help.hint_1: Use the exact final commit message.
help.hint_2: The final short status should print no file lines.

try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.solutionCode:
git commit -m "Prepare neighborhood guide handoff"
git log --oneline
git show --stat --oneline HEAD
git status --short
try_final_neighborhood_resource_guide_hi.instructions: Record the prepared maintenance snapshot with the message `Prepare neighborhood guide handoff`. Then inspect the concise history, the latest commit summary, and the final short status. The capstone should end with four focused commits and no unfinished tracked work.

finalCapstone.description: The capstone starts in `neighborhood-guide/` with no history from the other projects. File edits are still supplied exactly because this is a Git assessment. Your responsibility is to apply initialization, focused history, path maintenance, ignore rules, safe restore, and final verification across six cumulative steps.
finalCapstone.title: Complete the Neighborhood Guide Capstone

intro.title: Fresh Repository, Independent Transfer
intro.bodyMarkdown: The capstone starts in `neighborhood-guide/` with four small text files and no history from the other projects. Every required edit is supplied exactly because this remains a Git assessment. Across six cumulative steps, you will build focused history, manage paths, add an ignore policy, recover a mistake, and verify a clean handoff.
